# RECOVERY PLAN - Modular Strategy Optimization
# Generated by: AnalysisExpert Persona
# Date: 2026-04-19

================================================================================
I. THE "ASSASSIN" FILTERS (NEEDS LOOSENING)
These filters killed $59,042 in simulated profit.
================================================================================

1. Trapped Trader Logic (TrappedLongs / TrappedShorts)
   - CURRENT: Vetoes trades if any trapped volume is detected.
   - PROBLEM: Kills winners at a 66% rate.
   - RECOMMENDATION: Change from VETO to WEAK (Score deduction instead of hard stop). 
     Only VETO if TrappedVolume > 3x the 10-bar average.

2. Exhausted Cumulative Delta (ExhaustedCD)
   - CURRENT: Vetoes if CD is at extreme.
   - PROBLEM: Kills winners at a 63% rate.
   - RECOMMENDATION: Remove the VETO entirely. CD extremes in your current data 
     actually represent strong momentum, not exhaustion.

3. Hidden Order Flow (Iceberg/Sweep)
   - CURRENT: Vetoes if BrickWall or Iceberg is detected against the trade.
   - PROBLEM: Winners are punching through these walls 63% of the time.
   - RECOMMENDATION: Treat as a "Speed Bump." Reduce SignalScore by 10 points 
     instead of a full VETO.

================================================================================
II. THE "LEAKY" FILTERS (NEEDS TIGHTENING)
These are allowing losers through.
================================================================================

1. Delta Fomo Filter (CD Threshold)
   - OBSERVATION: Losers have 3.6x higher Cumulative Delta than winners.
   - RECOMMENDATION: Add a "Max Momentum" check. If CD > 1200, the trade is 
     likely over-extended. Do not enter.

2. Cumulative Delta Slope (DSL)
   - OBSERVATION: Winners have much lower DSL (-2.4) than losers (-1.0).
   - RECOMMENDATION: Require a more aggressive delta slope in the direction of 
     the trade. Tighten the DSL requirement by 30%.

3. Delta Swing High (DSH)
   - OBSERVATION: Winners have DSH of 5.9, losers have only 1.5.
   - RECOMMENDATION: If DSH < 3.0, the signal lacks the "structural thrust" 
     needed to reach the target. VETO these signals.

================================================================================
III. ARCHITECTURAL SUGGESTION (NO CODE CHANGE)
================================================================================
- Target the "Net Score 40-50" range.
- Avoid "Ultra-Confluence" (Score > 70). These trades are too obvious and 
  often result in late entries or reversals.
- Consider a "Time-Based Decay" for ORB signals. Your best winners hit 
  within 1-2 bars (BARS_TO_HIT). If it takes >4 bars, the edge vanishes.

================================================================================
IV. NEXT STEPS
================================================================================
1. Update `ConfluenceEngine.cs` to reflect the VETO -> WEAK transitions.
2. Update `SignalRankingEngine.cs` to penalize high CD (>1200).
3. Re-run backtest and use `deep_analysis.py` to verify the "Leaked Profit" 
   has been recovered.
